content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a computer game. There are NNN monsters on the number line.

Chef is at position 000 on the number line. Initially, the ithi^{th}ith monster has health equal to HiH_iHi‚Äč and is at the position AiA_iAi‚Äč on the line.

It is guaranteed that Ai<Ai+1A_i \lt A_{i+1}Ai‚Äč<Ai+1‚Äč for every 1‚Č§i<N1 \leq i \lt N1‚Č§i<N.

Every second, the following happens:


First, Chef can choose a single index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N) such that the ithi^{th}ith monster is still alive, and fire **one** bullet at it.

This reduces HiH_iHi‚Äč by 111.
Then, all alive monsters whose health is 000 will die.
Finally, all alive monsters move one position closer to Chef.
If any monster reaches position 000, Chef loses.

If Chef manages to kill all the monsters before any of them reach position 000, he wins.

Before the start of the game, Chef can plant a bomb at any position on the line.

If the bomb is placed at position XXX, all monsters whose starting positions lie between X‚ąíKX-KX‚ąíK and X+KX+KX+K will die immediately, and the game will start with the remaining monsters.

If Chef places the bomb optimally, can he win the game?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚ÄĒ the number of monsters and the range of the bomb, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the initial positions of the monsters.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers H1,H2,‚Ä¶,HNH_1,H_2,\ldots ,H_NH1‚Äč,H2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,HN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the initial health of the monsters.




Output Format
For each test case, print on a new line the answer: `YES` if Chef can kill all NNN monsters before they reach 000, and `NO` otherwise.


You can output each letter of the answer in either uppercase or lowercase. For example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `YES` will all be recognized as positive responses.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases will not exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef will plant the bomb at X=6X = 6X=6. This causes the monsters at positions 4,6,4, 6,4,6, and 999 to die immediately. Then,

At t=1t = 1t=1, Chef shoots the 1th1^{th}1th monster. This kills it.
Then, the fifth monster moves one step closer, to position 101010.
At t=2t = 2t=2, Chef shoots the 5th5^{th}5th monster. This kills it.
All the monsters are now dead, so Chef wins.

Test case 222: No matter where Chef plants the bomb, he cannot win.
